01_initial_request.user_intent: improve the reproducibility and quantitative accuracy of proteomics data
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: reproducibility and quantitative accuracy (coefficients of variation)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Conventional bottom-up proteomics requires cell lysis with SDS followed by detergent removal and protein solubilization before trypsin digestion. [Gap] The effect of denaturants on trypsin digestion efficiency of precipitated proteins has not been quantitatively evaluated. [Decision] To establish baseline performance, chose to use conventional proteome sample preparation method to perform cell lysis and digestion on HEK293T cells, expecting to obtain reference proteome data for comparison.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Proteins extracted with SDS, precipitated with acetone, and digested with trypsin after re-solubilization in various buffers

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteins precipitated with acetone after SDS solubilization are conventionally re-solubilized with denaturants before digestion. [Gap] It is unknown whether re-solubilization is necessary for complete enzymatic digestion. [Decision] To test digestion efficiency without denaturants, chose to use ammonium bicarbonate buffer to perform trypsin digestion on acetone-precipitated proteins without re-solubilization, expecting to obtain peptide digests for LC-MS analysis.

tool.name: Trypsin digestion in ammonium bicarbonate buffer

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Protein solution became transparent after digestion, suggesting trypsin digests proteins even in insoluble state

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Urea is commonly used for protein solubilization before enzymatic digestion. [Gap] The effect of urea on trypsin digestion efficiency compared to denaturant-free conditions is unknown. [Decision] To compare digestion efficiency, chose to use 0.8M urea buffer to perform trypsin digestion on acetone-precipitated proteins, expecting to obtain peptide digests for comparison.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Number of peptides identified did not differ substantially from denaturant-free condition, but trypsin activity slightly reduced

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] GdnHCl is a strong denaturant used for protein solubilization. [Gap] The effect of GdnHCl on trypsin digestion efficiency needs evaluation. [Decision] To compare digestion efficiency, chose to use 0.8M GdnHCl buffer to perform trypsin digestion on acetone-precipitated proteins, expecting to obtain peptide digests for comparison.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Remarkably less fully cleaved peptides identified; 2824 proteins showed significant abundance changes; trypsin activity reduced

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SDC-SLS surfactants are known to increase trypsin digestion efficiency. [Gap] The effect of SDC-SLS on digestion in this experimental system needs verification. [Decision] To compare digestion efficiency, chose to use 2.4mM SDC-SLS buffer to perform trypsin digestion on acetone-precipitated proteins, expecting to obtain peptide digests for comparison.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: No significant improvement in digestion efficiency observed compared to denaturant-free condition

references.relevance: Previous report that SDC-SLS increases trypsin digestion efficiency

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Multiple digestion conditions have been tested with different buffers. [Gap] Quantitative comparison of digestion efficiency across conditions requires computational analysis. [Decision] To evaluate digestion efficiency quantitatively, chose to use DIA-NN to perform library-free search on DIA data, expecting to obtain peptide and protein identification and quantification.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: More than 40,000 peptides composed of 6000 proteins identified; denaturant-free condition showed best reproducibility with smallest CV distribution

references.relevance: DIA-NN enables deep proteome coverage in high throughput

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Organic solvents are used for cell fixation but proteins typically need solubilization for biochemical analysis. [Gap] Protein recovery from cells fixed with various organic solvents or acids has not been quantified. [Decision] To evaluate protein recovery, chose to use BCA Protein Assay Kit to perform protein quantification on cells fixed with organic solvents and acids, expecting to obtain protein concentration measurements.

parameters.fixation_solvents: methanol, ethanol, 1-propanol, 2-propanol, acetone, acetonitrile, dimethyl formamide

02_agent_trajectory.observation: No significant difference in protein recovery compared to acetone precipitate of SDS-extracted proteins

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cells fixed with organic solvents could potentially be directly digested without cell lysis. [Gap] Acetone fixation effect on protein identification and digestion efficiency needs evaluation. [Decision] To test direct digestion feasibility, chose to use trypsin digestion to perform direct digestion on acetone-fixed HeLa cells without cell lysis, expecting to obtain peptide digests for LC-MS analysis.

tool.name: Direct trypsin digestion of acetone-fixed cells

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Number of identified proteins and peptides showed marked decrease compared to conventional method; 1083 proteins not detected; protein loss biased towards lower molecular weights

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Acetone fixation showed protein loss in direct digestion. [Gap] Methanol fixation may provide better protein recovery for direct digestion. [Decision] To establish optimal iSDAC method, chose to use trypsin digestion to perform direct digestion on methanol-fixed HeLa cells without cell lysis, expecting to obtain peptide digests comparable to conventional method.

tool.name: Direct trypsin digestion of methanol-fixed cells (iSDAC)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Number of identified proteins and peptides almost equivalent to conventional method; only 98 proteins not detected; high quantitative reproducibility achieved

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] iSDAC method has been established for cultured cells. [Gap] Quantitative comparison between iSDAC and conventional method requires computational analysis. [Decision] To evaluate iSDAC performance, chose to use DIA-NN to perform quantitative analysis on methanol-fixed cell digests, expecting to obtain protein abundance and CV measurements.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: CV distribution for protein abundance biassed towards small areas indicating high reproducibility; no significant artificial modifications induced by methanol fixation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] iSDAC does not involve protein quantification making enzyme-to-substrate ratio difficult to control. [Gap] The acceptable range of trypsin amounts for quantitative digestion needs determination. [Decision] To optimize trypsin amount, chose to use trypsin digestion to perform direct digestion with various enzyme-to-substrate ratios (1:200, 1:100, 1:50, 1:25, 1:12.5), expecting to identify optimal range for quantification.

tool.name: Trypsin digestion with varying enzyme-to-substrate ratios

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Enzyme-substrate ratios from 1:100 to 1:25 had negligible effect on protein level quantification; peptide level quantification of missed cleavage sites significantly affected

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] iSDAC has been validated on cultured cells. [Gap] Applicability to tissue samples needs verification for broader utility. [Decision] To extend iSDAC to tissues, chose to use trypsin digestion to perform direct digestion on methanol-fixed frozen mouse liver powder, expecting to obtain tissue proteome data comparable to conventional method.

parameters.trypsin_ratio: 1:50 (trypsin to protein estimated from wet weight)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: No substantial difference in number of identified peptides and proteins compared to conventional method; 4656 proteins commonly identified; high reproducibility achieved

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Tissue samples processed with iSDAC need quantitative validation. [Gap] Reproducibility and quantitative accuracy of iSDAC on tissue samples requires computational analysis. [Decision] To validate tissue iSDAC performance, chose to use DIA-NN to perform quantitative analysis on mouse liver digests, expecting to obtain CV measurements and protein abundance comparisons.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Smaller distribution of CVs for protein abundance in iSDAC samples; 22 proteins decreased (14 annotated as extracellular space including collagen)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Semi-tryptic peptides may indicate atypical digestion patterns. [Gap] Contribution of semi-tryptic peptides to results needs evaluation across digestion conditions. [Decision] To evaluate atypical digestion, chose to use FragPipe with MSFragger to perform semi-tryptic peptide identification, expecting to obtain semi-tryptic peptide counts and abundance data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Reasonable number of semi-tryptic peptides identified in denaturant-free condition; trypsin enzyme activity highest in absence of denaturant

references.relevance: MSFragger enables ultrafast and comprehensive peptide identification

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Peptide modifications may occur during sample preparation. [Gap] Effect of acetone or methanol fixation on peptide modifications needs characterization. [Decision] To evaluate artificial modifications, chose to use FragPipe with DIA-Umpire to perform modification analysis, expecting to obtain percentages of modified peptides.

parameters.modifications_searched: deamidation, didehydrogenation, dihydroxylation, demethylation, formylation, dethiomethylation, methylation, oxidation, pyroglutamate formation

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Methanol fixation did not significantly induce artificial modifications; acetone fixation increased percentages of modified peptides

references.relevance: DIA-Umpire provides comprehensive computational framework for DIA proteomics

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Data-independent acquisition (DIA) mass spectrometry with DIA-NN quantitative analysis

protein_identification.interpretation: Commonly identified proteins between iSDAC and conventional method in mouse liver, showing comparable coverage

peptide_identification.interpretation: Total peptides identified across digestion conditions, indicating comprehensive proteome coverage

reproducibility_CV.interpretation: CV distribution for protein abundance biassed towards small areas in iSDAC, indicating high quantitative reproducibility

methanol_fixed_protein_loss.interpretation: Only 98 proteins not detected by methanol fixation compared to conventional method, minimal loss

acetone_fixed_protein_loss.interpretation: 1083 proteins not found in acetone-fixed cells, showing acetone is suboptimal for iSDAC

optimal_trypsin_ratio.interpretation: Range with negligible effect on protein level quantification analysis

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The iSDAC method successfully achieved the research objective. Direct trypsin digestion of methanol-fixed cells achieved the same digestion efficiency and quantitative reproducibility as the conventional method, with high quantitative accuracy (small CV distribution) and improved reproducibility. The method simplifies sample preparation by eliminating cell lysis and detergent removal steps while maintaining proteome-wide coverage. The method is applicable to both cultured cells and tissue samples (mouse liver), demonstrating versatility. The quantifiable goal of improving reproducibility and quantitative accuracy was met, as evidenced by comparable protein identification numbers and smaller CV distributions compared to conventional methods.
